issue.title: [Bug]: Kiro missing from agentLabels.ts (no display label / alias)
issue.body:
## Before you submit
- [x] I searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate.
- [x] I replaced the example text with the real behavior, reproduction steps, expected result, and version.

## What happened?
`apps/web/src/utils/agentLabels.ts` has no entry for **Kiro**, so Kiro is not treated as a first-class agent in the web UI the way Claude, Codex, and Devin are.

Concretely, in `AGENT_LABELS`:
- there is **no** `kiro: 'Kiro'` (or `'Kiro CLI'`) label, and
- in `AGENT_ALIASES` there is **no** `'kiro cli': 'kiro'` alias.

Because of this, `exactAgentDisplayName('kiro')` returns `null`, and `agentDisplayName('kiro', …)` falls through to `safeFallbackLabel`, rendering a raw/normalized string instead of the canonical brand name. Every consumer of these helpers is affected — e.g. `agentModelDisplayName` (used to show "Agent · model") and any UI that resolves an agent's display name (handoff/agent chips, run headers).

Note the brand icon already exists at `apps/web/public/agent-icons/kiro.svg`, so this is purely a missing label/alias mapping, not a missing asset. Kiro is otherwise a fully supported runtime (`apps/daemon/src/runtimes/defs/kiro.ts`, registered in `runtimes/registry.ts`, metadata → `https://kiro.dev`).

For comparison, `AGENT_LABELS` currently contains `claude: 'Claude'`, `codex: 'Codex'`, `devin: 'Devin'`, `antigravity: 'Antigravity'`, etc., but not `kiro`.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Run an Open Design session using the **Kiro CLI** agent (agent id `kiro`).
2. Look at any UI surface that shows the agent's display name (agent chip / run header / "Agent · model" label).
3. Observe the label is a raw fallback (e.g. `kiro` / `Kiro CLI` passed through) rather than a canonical, alias-normalized brand label like the other agents get.

Alternatively, in code: `exactAgentDisplayName('kiro')` returns `null`, whereas `exactAgentDisplayName('codex')` returns `'Codex'`.

## Expected behavior
`agentLabels.ts` maps Kiro like the other first-class agents:
- `AGENT_LABELS`: add `kiro: 'Kiro'` (label consistent with the other short brand names).
- `AGENT_ALIASES`: add `'kiro cli': 'kiro'` (and `'kiro-cli': 'kiro'`) so the daemon's `name: 'Kiro CLI'` / `bin: 'kiro-cli'` resolve to the canonical id.

After the fix, `exactAgentDisplayName('kiro')` / `agentDisplayName('kiro', 'Kiro CLI')` return `'Kiro'`, and `agentIconId('kiro')` resolves to the existing `kiro.svg` brand asset. No regression to existing agents.

## Open Design version
0.12.1

## Platform
Other — UI label mapping, not OS-specific.

## Logs (optional)
```shell
# Not applicable — no runtime error; incorrect/generic display label only.
```

## Screenshots (optional)
Attach a screenshot of a Kiro run's agent chip / header showing the non-canonical label.

## Additional context
- The same file is also missing labels for other newer ACP agents (hermes, kilo, kimi, vibe, pi, grok/grok-build, trae/trae-cli, reasonix). This issue is scoped to **Kiro**; the others can be a follow-up.
- Part of a broader effort to make Kiro a true first-class equivalent of Claude Code and Codex across the product (see also the MCP dropdown bug and the `od mcp install kiro` request).

outcome.label: lost_race
outcome.detail: Another contributor claimed + opened an identical-fix PR (#5085) while John's clone was still downloading; John's finished, review-gated fix was never pushed (zero external footprint).

fidelityNote: Race timing is not observable from a point-in-time vet snapshot — oss-scout has no signal for 'a competing contributor is about to claim this in the next N minutes'. Excluded from headline accuracy; reported in the lost-race appendix.
